directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.
directives.name: include

directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.
directives.name: skip

args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted in [Markdown](https://daringfireball.net/projects/markdown/).
args.name: reason

directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.
directives.name: deprecated

types.description:
Global parameters
=============================

__sort__: a column name with a preceding sign (-/+)

    + : values increasing (default)
        "sort": "+asn" or "sort": "asn"
    - : values decreasing
        "sort": "-asn"

 If we only allow multi object selections, this allows us
 to include the meta data in all response.  It basically

 The GraphQLBundle places everything in a wrapper
```
 {
    "data":{
```

"General arguments"
------------------------------

__offset__: offset to the number of items from the front of the list. Default is 0

__first__: the first number of items from the offset. Default is 500.

__limit (internal parameter)__: maximum number of results per query is 10 000.

__date__: returns the value at a single date

__startDate, endDate__: define the time interval you want so search in

   - Time interval is "inclusive" which means "t BETWEEN starDate AND endDate" is equivalent to "t >= starDate AND t <= endDate"
   - By default (if no date interval is specified) the latest month data is shown
   - Examples: 
       - date: "2020-03-01"
       - dateStart:"2018-12-01", dateEnd:"2020-02-01"
   - Sorting by "date" is supported. The default sorting is by date in ascending order.

 __seen__: list based on if the item was seen in BGP
- __false__ - only show item which were not seen
- __true__ - only show item which were seen
- __null__ -  all item

 __sort__: defaults to rank

fields.description:
All of the following return data for the most recent dataset

### datasets arguments
- __asn__: the ASN that will be returned
fields.name: datasets

###
__asn__:  Autonomous System (AS) (which approximately map to Internet Service Provider)

- announcing.numberAddresses

- cone.numberAddresses

fields.description:
### asnLink arguments
the ordering of asn0 and asn1 doesn't matter.
fields.name: asnLink

fields.description:
### asnLinks arguments

__asn__: links that contain asn

__asns__: links that contain any asn in asn list

__sort__: defaults to date, relationship, then rank
- date
- relationship
- rank
- asn (if __asn__ is set, sort by the second asn)
- cone.numberAsns (if __asn__ is set, sort by the second asn's
  customer cone)
fields.name: asnLinks

fields.description:
### AsnCone or the customer cone of AS X is defined as the set of ASes that X can reach using p2c links; for AS X the customer cone includes X's customers, as well as X's customers' customers, and so on 
### AsnCone arguments

__asn__: Asn whose cone is returned
fields.name: asnCone

fields.description:
### asnConesStream arguments
__asns__: list of ASNs whose cone should be returned (or)

__asnMembersAll__: list of asns which must be members of the customer cone (all)

__asnMembersSome__: list of asns which must be members of the customer cone (at least some)

__prefixMembers__: list of prefixes which must be members of the customer cone (all)

__pageCursor__: cursor imitates graphql connection pagination, allowing you to iterate over the list of external objects (cone) and the asns and prefixes objects enclosed in it as one continuous list. It contains digits separated by a comma, in which the first digit is the pointer to the current Cone Id, the second digit is the Asn index in the Asns list belonging to the current Cone and the third digit is the Prefix index in the Asns list belonging to the current Cone (if its empty it tells that the cursor has not yet started navigating the Prefixes nested list , because it did not complete the passage through the Asns object)
fields.name: asnConesStream

types.description:
The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8
character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to
represent free-form human-readable text.
types.name: String

types.description: Dataset must be documented
types.name: Dataset

types.description:
The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric
values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.
types.name: Int

The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional
values as specified by
[IEEE 754](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.description: Users who want the meta data can use the Connection
types.name: Page_Info

types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.
types.name: Boolean

types.description:
Asn structure
- rank - is ASN's rank, which is based on it's customer cone size, which in turn is inferred from BGP paths by CAIDA's AS relationships inference algorithm
- source - source of the whois meta data. Typically it is one of the Regional Internet Registries (RIRs)
- name - name of the ASN
- longitude of the ASN
- latitude of the ASN
- organization the ASN belongs to
- asnDegree is degree of the asn
- country which the ASN is in
- announcing - set of prefixes announced by the ASN
- asnLinks are the links the ASN is connected to other ASNs
- cone is the cone the ASN belongs to
- ixp - is true if the ASN is used by an Internet eXchange Point (IXP)
- seen - is true when ASN is seen in BGP
- cliqueMember - is true if the ASN is inferred to be a member of the clique of ASN at the top of the ASN hierarchy
types.name: Asn

types.description: Announcing
types.name: Announcing

types.description:
Organization structure
- date - rank of the organization
- id - id of the Organization
- orgId - id of the Organization
- orgName-name of the organization
- rank - rank of the organization
- seen 
- country- country which the Organization is in
- asnDegree - degree of te ASN, which is the number of neighbors that a ASN has
- orgDegree -degree of the organization, which is the number of neighbors that an Organization has
- asnLinks are the links of the Organizaion members to other ASNs
- cone is the cone the Organization belongs to
- neighbors - organizations having direct links to the queried organization
- members - are ASN members of the organization
types.name: Organization

types.description:
The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to
refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON
response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable.
When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer
(such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.
types.name: ID

types.description: AsnLink structure
types.name: AsnLink

types.description: Location structure
types.name: Location

types.description:
Cone structure
- asn - largest ASN of the cone
- cone - information about the cone: number of ASNs, pefixes and addresses
- asns - ASN members of the cone
- prefixes - prefix members of the cone
types.name: AsnCone

types.description:
Prefix structure
- network - the network position of the address.
- length - the number of bits that are part of the network.
- origin - the ASN which announced the prefix to Border Gateway Protocol (BGP)
types.name: Prefix

types.description: Streamed cones
types.name: StreamPageInfo

types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.
fields.name: types

fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.
fields.name: queryType

fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.
fields.name: mutationType

fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.
fields.name: subscriptionType

fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.
fields.name: directives

types.name: __Schema

types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name and description, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.
types.name: __Type

types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.
enumValues.name: SCALAR

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.
enumValues.name: OBJECT

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields` and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.
enumValues.name: INTERFACE

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.
enumValues.name: UNION

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.
enumValues.name: ENUM

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.
enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.
enumValues.name: LIST

enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.
enumValues.name: NON_NULL

types.name: __TypeKind

types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.
types.name: __Field

types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.
fields.name: defaultValue

types.name: __InputValue

types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.
types.name: __EnumValue

types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.
types.name: __Directive

types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.
enumValues.name: QUERY

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.
enumValues.name: MUTATION

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.
enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.
enumValues.name: FIELD

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.
enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.
enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.
enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.
enumValues.name: SCHEMA

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.
enumValues.name: SCALAR

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.
enumValues.name: OBJECT

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.
enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.
enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.
enumValues.name: INTERFACE

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.
enumValues.name: UNION

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.
enumValues.name: ENUM

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.
enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.
enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT

enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.
enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
